allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives abstraction the whole-machine view that lets high-level models capture interrelationships instead of static detail piles.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking is intrinsically allied with abstraction

allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking keeps abstraction honest by rebuilding the model from facts rather than from inherited labels alone.
allies.source_quote: abstraction conflicts with first-principles-thinking when high-level labels hide untested assumptions

allies.rationale_text: Simplification is the practical expression of abstraction when complexity is compressed into a clearer structure without losing what matters.

antagonists.rationale_text: Theory-induced-blindness corrupts abstraction when the framework becomes a cage that forces reality to fit the model rather than revising the model to fit reality.
antagonists.source_quote: torture reality so that it fits your models

structured_tensions.tension_text: Abstraction conflicts with first-principles-thinking when high-level labels hide untested assumptions that should have been stripped away before synthesis.
structured_tensions.source_quote: abstraction vs first-principles-thinking: abstraction conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when high-level labels hide untested assumptions.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on systems and simplification allies plus one explicit tension around hiding assumptions, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the source-backed systems, simplification, and anti-framework-overreach surface.
open_questions: Analogical-thinking is source-real, but it was omitted because the current graph vocabulary does not expose a matching clean target id.
